title: Attention Is All You Need
summary: Introduced the Transformer architecture, replacing recurrence entirely with multi-head self-attention. The key innovations: scaled dot-product attention, multi-head attention (allowing the model to attend to different representation subspaces), positional encoding (since the model has no inherent notion of sequence order), and the encoder-decoder structure with residual connections and layer normalization. Originally designed for machine translation, the architecture turned out to be the universal substrate for modern AI.
key_contributions: Introduced the Transformer architecture — the foundation of all modern LLMs
Replaced recurrence with self-attention, enabling massive parallelization
Multi-head attention allows attending to multiple representation subspaces
Positional encoding solves the sequence ordering problem without recurrence
Achieved new SOTA on English-German and English-French translation
impact: The most consequential architecture paper in AI history. Every frontier model — GPT, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA, Mixtral — is built on the Transformer. The paper has over 130,000 citations and counting.
query_hooks: What is the most cited AI paper?
